body.thing: Granny dispatch-health WARN (available builder idle past threshold while a backlog waits)
body.intention: An available builder idle past threshold while work waits surfaces a WARN operators can see, instead of Granny cycling silently for 16h

red_run.summary:
def test_idle_builder_with_deferred_work_warns():
        """The observed stall: Aider.0 available + idle 16h, 217 tickets deferred to an
        unavailable CC.0, 0 targeted at Aider. Work waits; it isn't reaching the idle
        builder. That MUST surface as a WARN — this is the whole point of the ticket."""
        report = summarize_dispatch_health(
            [BuilderState(name="Aider.0", available=True, last_dispatch_age_s=_16H)],
            dispatchable_by_target=0,
            deferred_unavailable=217,
            idle_threshold_s=_IDLE_THRESHOLD,
        )
>       assert report.warns, "an available builder idle 16h with 217 tickets waiting must WARN"
E       AssertionError: an available builder idle 16h with 217 tickets waiting must WARN
E       assert []
E        +  where [] = HealthReport(info_line='', warns=[]).warns

tests/test_granny_dispatch_health.py:41: AssertionError
=========================== short test summary info ============================
FAILED tests/test_granny_dispatch_health.py::test_idle_builder_with_deferred_work_warns

body.why: Granny ran 16h with Aider.0 idle and 217 tickets deferred and nothing signalled it (Akien had to read the tmux pane); the idle-builder WARN is the missing glanceable signal.
